participants.name: Deion Burks
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Stood on combine numbers for 40 (4.30), vertical (42.5), broad (131"), bench (26 reps). Ran shuttle and cone at pro day to improve on combine. Did not run shuttle or cone at combine.

participants.name: Keontez Lewis
participants.notes: Not a combine invitee. Pro day was his only pre-draft showcase opportunity. Specific drill results not publicly reported; participated in varying capacities.

participants.name: Jaydn Ott
participants.notes: Not a combine invitee. Transferred from Cal. Pro day was his primary pre-draft showcase. 40.5-inch vertical would have been 2nd among RBs at combine. Broad jump would have tied for 1st among RBs.

participants.name: Jaren Kanak
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Stood on combine numbers for 40, vertical (36"), broad (9'11"), bench (24 reps). Ran shuttle and cone at pro day. Converted from linebacker to tight end.

participants.name: Owen Heinecke
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Stood on combine numbers for 40 (4.62), cone (7.15), and others. Only re-tested shuttle at pro day (4.25 would have ranked 3rd among LBs at combine). Also did positional work. Former lacrosse walk-on.

participants.name: Kendal Daniels
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Tested at pro day as listed by PFF. Transferred from Oklahoma State. Listed as LB by Oklahoma but has safety background.

participants.name: R Mason Thomas
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Did not go through any testing at pro day, only positional drills. Stood on all combine numbers (40: 4.67, 10-yd: 1.63). Measurements from combine.

participants.name: Marvin Jones Jr.
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Combine 40: 4.69. Specific pro day drill results not publicly reported; participated in positional drills.

participants.name: Gracen Halton
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Completed 25 reps on bench press at pro day, then tweaked hamstring and was sidelined for the rest of the day. Combine results: 40=4.82, vertical=36.5", broad=9'6", shuttle=4.79, cone=8.07. Drawing Jets and Giants interest.

participants.name: Damonic Williams
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Combine bench: 30 reps (2nd among DTs). Tested broad jump (8'4") and shuttle (4.98) at pro day per PFF.

participants.name: Febechi Nwaiwu
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Specific pro day drill results not publicly reported. Participated in positional drills. Listed at 6-4, 319 lbs with 34 1/2-inch arms. Second-team All-SEC in 2025. Primarily played guard, also started at center.

participants.name: Derek Simmons
participants.notes: Not a combine invitee. Pro day was his only pre-draft showcase opportunity. Specific drill results not publicly reported; participated in varying capacities.

participants.name: Robert Spears-Jennings
participants.notes: Combine invitee. Combine 40: 4.32 (2nd among safeties). Tested vertical (38"), cone (7.12), and bench (14) at pro day per PFF. Measured 6-2, 205 lbs at combine.

participants.name: Austin Welch
participants.notes: Not a combine invitee. Participated as kicker; specific testing drill results not publicly reported. Did kicking drills.

additional_notes: Oklahoma held its 2026 Pro Day on Thursday, March 12 at the Everest Indoor Training Center in Norman, with scouts and representatives from all 32 NFL teams in attendance. Fourteen former Sooners participated. Ten of the 14 were NFL Combine invitees, and several of those players opted to stand on their combine numbers for most drills, only re-testing select events at pro day. R Mason Thomas did not test in any drills at pro day and only did positional work. Gracen Halton tweaked his hamstring after bench press and was done for the day. QB John Mateer (returning to OU for 2026 season) threw passes during positional drills but was not a pro day participant. Keontez Lewis, Jaydn Ott, Derek Simmons, and Austin Welch were not combine invites and used pro day as their primary showcase. Major Melson and Will Huggins (also draft-eligible) did NOT participate in the pro day. The 247Sports detailed results article is behind a paywall, which limits the availability of complete individual drill numbers for all 14 participants. PFF's tracker provided the most comprehensive publicly available pro day-specific testing data.
